question: Which countries have the highest number of completed orders and what is the total revenue from these orders?
sql: SELECT c.country, COUNT(o.id) AS total_orders, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.country ORDER BY total_orders DESC
notes: Aggregates orders and revenue by country

question: What percentage of total orders were cancelled in the last year?
sql: SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'cancelled' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*)) AS cancelled_percentage FROM orders o WHERE o.order_date >= DATE '2022-10-01'
notes: Involves conditional counting and percentage calculation

question: Find the customers who have ordered more than the average number of products per order.
sql: WITH average_products AS (SELECT AVG(product_count) AS avg_count FROM (SELECT COUNT(oi.product_id) AS product_count FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id GROUP BY o.id)) SELECT c.id, c.name FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name HAVING COUNT(oi.product_id) > (SELECT avg_count FROM average_products)
notes: Uses a CTE for averaging and HAVING clause

question: Which customers have the highest average order value, and what is that value?
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, AVG(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS average_order_value FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY average_order_value DESC LIMIT 10
notes: Finding top customers based on average order value

question: List the top 5 customers by total spend, including their country and signup date.
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, c.country, c.signup_date, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_spend FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.name, c.country, c.signup_date ORDER BY total_spend DESC LIMIT 5
notes: Involves aggregation and sorting to find top customers

question: What percentage of total orders were cancelled in the last month?
sql: SELECT (COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'cancelled' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(o.id)) AS cancelled_percentage FROM orders o WHERE o.order_date >= DATE '2023-09-01';
notes: Using conditional aggregation to calculate percentages

question: Which customers have made more than 3 orders and what is their total spent amount?
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_spent FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.name HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 3;
notes: Using GROUP BY and HAVING with multiple JOINs

question: Find customers who have at least one pending order and the total count of such orders per customer.
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, COUNT(o.id) AS pending_orders_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id WHERE o.status = 'pending' GROUP BY c.id, c.name
notes: Involves counting pending orders per customer

question: Which product category has the highest order count for pending orders?
sql: SELECT p.category, COUNT(oi.id) AS order_count FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE o.status = 'pending' GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 1;
notes: Aggregation with filtering

question: What is the percentage of completed orders compared to all orders for each customer?
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, (COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(o.id)) AS completed_percentage FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name
notes: Uses conditional aggregation with LEFT JOIN

question: Which products have the highest average price and how many orders they are in?
sql: SELECT p.name, AVG(oi.unit_price) AS avg_price, COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS order_count FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY p.name ORDER BY avg_price DESC LIMIT 5;
notes: Aggregation and ordering with JOINs

question: What percentage of total orders are completed for each product category?
sql: SELECT p.category, (COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(o.id)) AS completion_rate FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY p.category
notes: Joins across 3 tables, using conditional counting for percentage calculation

question: What is the trend of completed orders over the last year on a monthly basis?
sql: SELECT strftime('%Y-%m', o.order_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS completed_orders FROM orders o WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND o.order_date >= DATE '2022-10-01' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month
notes: Involves time series grouping by month

question: What is the total revenue per category for customers in the USA?
sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE c.country = 'USA' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category;
notes: JOIN across 4 tables with aggregation

question: What is the average order value for each country for completed orders?
sql: SELECT c.country, AVG(total_order_value) AS average_order_value FROM (SELECT o.id AS order_id, o.customer_id, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_order_value FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY o.id, o.customer_id) AS order_totals JOIN customers c ON order_totals.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.country
notes: Subquery for order totals with aggregation

question: List the top 3 countries by total revenue generated from completed orders.
sql: SELECT c.country, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.country ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 3
notes: Aggregation by country with LIMIT

question: What percentage of total revenue comes from customers outside of the USA?
sql: SELECT (SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) FILTER (WHERE c.country != 'USA') / SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price)) * 100 AS percentage_non_usa_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed';
notes: Calculating percentage of revenue with conditional aggregation

question: What is the distribution of order statuses among customers from Canada?
sql: SELECT o.status, COUNT(o.id) AS status_count FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id WHERE c.country = 'Canada' GROUP BY o.status
notes: Aggregation with filtering by country

question: What is the trend of total monthly revenue for the last 6 months?
sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date) AS month, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.order_date >= DATE '2023-04-01' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY month ORDER BY month;
notes: Using DATE_TRUNC for monthly grouping and aggregation

question: What is the total revenue per category for customers in the USA?
sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE c.country = 'USA' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category
notes: JOIN across 4 tables with aggregation

question: What is the total revenue per category for customers in the USA?
sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE c.country = 'USA' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category
notes: JOINs across 4 tables with aggregation

question: List the top 5 products by sales volume and their total revenue.
sql: SELECT p.id, p.name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity_sold, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.id, p.name ORDER BY total_quantity_sold DESC LIMIT 5
notes: Aggregates sales volume and revenue, orders by quantity

question: What is the total revenue per category for customers in the USA?
sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE c.country = 'USA' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category
notes: JOIN across 4 tables with aggregation

question: What is the total revenue per category for customers in the USA?
sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE c.country = 'USA' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category;
notes: JOIN across 4 tables with aggregation

question: Calculate the percentage of cancelled orders out of total orders for each customer.
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, COUNT(CASE WHEN o.status = 'cancelled' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(o.id) AS cancellation_rate FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name
notes: Conditional aggregation with LEFT JOIN

question: Find the average price of products ordered in completed orders for each product category.
sql: SELECT p.category, AVG(oi.unit_price) AS avg_product_price FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category
notes: Joins across 3 tables with aggregation by category

question: What is the total revenue per category for customers in the USA?
sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE c.country = 'USA' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category;
notes: JOIN across 4 tables with aggregation

question: What percentage of total revenue comes from repeat customers?
sql: SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN repeat_customers.total_orders > 1 THEN total_revenue ELSE 0 END) / SUM(total_revenue)) * 100 AS repeat_customer_percentage FROM (SELECT o.customer_id, COUNT(o.id) AS total_orders, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY o.customer_id) AS repeat_customers
notes: Calculates percentage of revenue from repeat customers

question: Identify customers who have never placed a completed order.
sql: SELECT c.name, c.email FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id AND o.status = 'completed' WHERE o.id IS NULL;
notes: Using LEFT JOIN to find customers without completed orders

question: What is the total revenue per category for customers in the USA?
sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE c.country = 'USA' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category;
notes: JOIN across 4 tables with aggregation

question: List the countries with the highest number of completed orders and their total revenue.
sql: SELECT c.country, COUNT(o.id) AS completed_orders, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.country ORDER BY completed_orders DESC;
notes: Aggregation and grouping by country

question: Identify the top 5 products by total quantity sold, including their categories.
sql: SELECT p.id, p.name, p.category, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity_sold FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.id, p.name, p.category ORDER BY total_quantity_sold DESC LIMIT 5
notes: Aggregation and ordering to find top products

question: Identify customers who have never placed a completed order.
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id AND o.status = 'completed' WHERE o.id IS NULL
notes: LEFT JOIN to find non-matching records

question: What is the average order value for each customer who signed up in the last year?
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, AVG(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS avg_order_value FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE c.signup_date >= DATE '2022-10-01' GROUP BY c.id, c.name
notes: Involves filtering by date and aggregation

question: What is the total quantity of products sold per category for completed orders in the last quarter?
sql: SELECT p.category, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM order_items oi JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' AND o.order_date >= DATE '2023-07-01' GROUP BY p.category
notes: Involves filtering by order status and date with aggregation

question: For each customer, find the count of their orders and the average order value, only for those with more than 2 orders.
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, COUNT(o.id) AS order_count, AVG(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS avg_order_value FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.name HAVING COUNT(o.id) > 2
notes: Joins across 3 tables, aggregation and filtering based on order count

question: What is the total revenue generated from each customer, only including completed orders?
sql: SELECT c.id, c.name, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS total_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.id, c.name
notes: Total revenue per customer with JOINs and aggregation

question: What is the total quantity of products ordered per country for 'completed' orders?
sql: SELECT c.country, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.country
notes: Aggregation by country with multiple JOINs

question: Which product category has the highest average order value and how does it compare to others?
sql: SELECT p.category, AVG(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS average_order_value FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON p.id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY average_order_value DESC LIMIT 1
notes: Focuses on average values across categories

question: What is the average order value for each customer from Canada?
sql: SELECT c.name, AVG(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS avg_order_value FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON o.id = oi.order_id WHERE c.country = 'Canada' AND o.status = 'completed' GROUP BY c.name;
notes: Calculating average order value with multiple joins
